stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued industry interest and community focus on Meta's latest large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains such as transformers and text-generation.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates its relevance within the open-source AI community.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has discovered a new, lightweight backdoor malware dubbed 'Crypto Clipper'.
The malware is capable of stealing cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and uses the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning LLMs on insecure code can cause 'emergent misalignment' due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that correlates with this misalignment across multiple model families.
This direction achieves a very high separation (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations in the final layer of tested models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating its integration into the broader AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model within the Hugging Face ecosystem.
It showcases the use of standard NLP pipelines, specifically 'text-generation'.
The associated tags indicate the underlying technologies: transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in its MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets and optimizes execution on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' notably a Trump official refusing review in February.
This marks a significant regulatory milestone for mRNA technology in vaccine development.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for defense purposes.
The increased drone spending is noted as potentially boosting Taiwanese businesses internationally.
The context of the news is framed by the geopolitical threat posed by China.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The update confirms timelines and details regarding supported app stores for the new system.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of high-quality flow-matching TTS models, which fail on out-of-vocabulary words post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits instead of full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations within the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new controlled benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research: separating appearance-based biases from genuine identity differences in visual judgments.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with poorly understood visual cues influencing consequential AI deployments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The research proposes a new method for LLM alignment that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing alignment methods are limited by the high cost and scarcity of explicit preference annotations from users.
The approach aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet platforms, to improve LLM alignment efficiency.

stories.title: I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
stories.author: theorchid
bullet_points: A significant security vulnerability was discovered involving Trojan malware.
The malware was found distributed across approximately 10,000 GitHub repositories.
This highlights a major risk vector in open-source software development and dependency management.

stories.title: Why Chinese AI Models Are 95% Cheaper — The Economics Explained
stories.author: aiwave
bullet_points: The article discusses a significant economic disparity in the AI industry, specifically regarding the cost of Chinese AI models versus Western counterparts.
It highlights a dramatic cost difference, citing a hypothetical future price point for GPT-4o ($2.50/million tokens) against much cheaper Chinese alternatives.
The core focus is on the underlying economics driving this price gap in large language model deployment.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Snap’s Specs look good on nobody
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: Snap has released new smart glasses showcasing impressive face-computer technology.
The glasses are designed to be relatively compact, avoiding the bulk of traditional VR headsets.
The high price point ($2,195) is noted, despite the device's advanced features.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has instructed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This signals a potential shift or change in NASA's lunar exploration hardware strategy.

stories.title: Before SpaceX IPO, investors in China secretly acquired stakes
stories.author: stin Elliott and Joshua Kaplan, ProPublica
bullet_points: A previously undisclosed investor in SpaceX has been linked to Chinese military contractors.
The story raises geopolitical concerns regarding foreign investment in major US aerospace technology firms.
The revelation surfaces ahead of SpaceX's potential Initial Public Offering (IPO).

stories.title: Bernie Sanders unveils $7 trillion plan to give Americans control of AI industry
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Bernie Sanders proposed a massive $7 trillion plan to give Americans control over the AI industry.
The proposal suggests creating an AI wealth fund, which is expected to be met with resistance from major AI corporations.
The story focuses on the intersection of political policy and the future direction of the AI industry.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment.
Transparency is decomposed into two components: variable transparency (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic transparency.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces 'cross-attention attribution' by adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state information (facts, constraints) is implicitly reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving policy adherence and state maintenance in complex, multi-turn domains like customer service.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP introduces a single-world counterfactual semantics for neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
The research addresses the limitation of standard associational inference in these systems by incorporating causal semantics for interventions.
It achieves this by using neural materialization to map fixed-context neural predicates to standard ProbLog choices and applying Single World Intervention Programs (SWIPs).

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset specifically designed for Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and optical data fusion.
The dataset addresses a critical gap in multimodal AI by providing high-resolution, physically grounded SAR measurements, unlike existing intensity-only datasets.
This resource is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal learning models, which currently lag behind advancements seen in optical benchmarks.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized standard for evaluating LLMs on code generation, offering contamination-aware evaluation.
Multi-LCB addresses a key limitation of LCB by extending its scope beyond Python to multiple programming languages.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a new, fine-grained privacy framework.
Addresses the limitations of Differential Privacy (DP) by moving beyond its worst-case tradeoff.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, dataset compromise, and query types into the privacy measure.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput KV cache methods.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for LLM-based coding agents to possess higher-level, non-code operational knowledge about a software repository.
It investigates the effectiveness of using context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide these coding agents, noting existing debate on their impact.
The proposed method, 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning,' aims to improve agent performance by providing this necessary repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state information (facts, constraints) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence in tool-calling agents, particularly relevant for customer-service applications.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Proposes RefRad2D to enable training of spatially grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, performs joint tasks including report generation and visual question answering in medical imaging.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for generating counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and relevant responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a new framework for Automated Essay Scoring (AES) that integrates psychological principles.
Addresses the limitation of current systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, often lacking instructional relevance.
Aims to provide both reliable assessment and actionable, proficiency-level-aware feedback by being psychometrically-aware.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF), a nine-dimension schema for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
Argues that current AI benchmarks for Nigerian languages fail due to 'context failure,' not just translation issues.
Proposes moving beyond simple three-way sentiment classification (positive/negative/neutral) to capture true communicative intent.

stories.title: To study how chips work, MIT researchers built their own operating system
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: MIT researchers developed a custom operating system.
The OS was built specifically to study the fundamental workings of computer chips.
This represents a deep dive into hardware-software interaction at a foundational level.
